articles.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
articles.summary: Elon Musk sues Sam Altman and OpenAI, claiming the company abandoned its nonprofit mission to develop beneficial AI for profit instead. The high-stakes trial began April 27th with jury selection and could reshape OpenAI's future.

articles.title: Meta is running get-rich-quick ads for its AI tools
articles.summary: Meta's Manus AI subsidiary is running get-rich-quick ads promising easy money by using AI to build websites for businesses and selling them. The campaign paid content creators to promote the scheme on social media.

articles.title: Gemini is rolling out to cars with Google built-in
articles.summary: Google is rolling out its Gemini AI assistant to vehicles with Google built-in, upgrading from Google Assistant. The update promises improved natural conversations, vehicle-specific information access, and settings adjustments.

articles.title: Salesforce is crowdsourcing its AI roadmap — with customers
articles.summary: Salesforce crowdsources its AI roadmap from customers, believing that problems faced by one enterprise are likely shared by others.

articles.title: X announces a rebuilt ad platform powered by AI
articles.summary: X launches an AI-powered rebuilt ads platform to boost revenue growth. The new system aims to improve advertising effectiveness and user experience.

articles.title: Meta says its business AI now facilitates 10 million conversations a week
articles.summary: Meta's business AI now facilitates 10 million conversations weekly, with over 8 billion advertisers having used at least one of its GenAI tools.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million in Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure competing against AWS and Google Cloud.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges $20-$200/month for AI coding assistance, but free alternative Goose offers similar functionality as open-source software running locally with no subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard hiring campaign in San Francisco. The startup used encoded AI tokens and a coding challenge to recruit engineers, with 430 people solving the puzzle.

articles.title: Why a recent supply-chain attack singled out security firms Checkmarx and Bitwarden
articles.summary: Security firm Checkmarx suffered multiple supply-chain attacks in six weeks, including a Trivy scanner compromise and direct GitHub account breach that delivered malware to customers seeking credentials.

articles.title: Open source package with 1 million monthly downloads stole user credentials
articles.summary: A popular open-source package with 1M monthly downloads was compromised, allowing attackers to steal user credentials including API tokens, SSH keys, and cloud provider credentials through a malicious version.

articles.title: Why are top university websites serving porn? It comes down to shoddy housekeeping.
articles.summary: Prestigious university websites including Berkeley, Columbia, and Washington University are serving pornography and malware through compromised subdomains due to poor record-keeping by administrators. Hundreds of subdomains across 34+ universities have been exploited by scammers.

articles.title: This startup’s new mechanistic interpretability tool lets you debug LLMs
articles.summary: Goodfire released Silico, a tool allowing researchers to debug LLMs by adjusting parameters during training. It offers fine-grained control over model development, making AI creation more scientific and less mysterious.

articles.title: The Download: the North Pole’s future and humanoid data
articles.summary: Scientists are drilling beneath the Arctic seabed to investigate the North Pole's past ice conditions and understand rapid climate changes affecting the Arctic Ocean.

articles.title: The Download: storing nuclear waste and orchestrating agents
articles.summary: Nuclear energy gains political support and tech investment, but the US urgently needs a permanent solution for 2,000 metric tons of annual high-level waste with no current storage plan.

articles.title: AI evals are becoming the new compute bottleneck
articles.summary: As AI models advance, evaluating their performance is becoming more computationally expensive than training. This evaluation bottleneck is emerging as a critical constraint in AI development.

articles.title: Granite 4.1 LLMs: How They’re Built
articles.summary: Granite 4.1 LLMs are built using advanced transformer architecture with optimized training techniques. The models focus on efficiency and performance across various enterprise applications.

articles.title: DeepInfra on Hugging Face Inference Providers 🔥
articles.summary: DeepInfra has been added to Hugging Face's inference providers, enabling developers to access high-performance AI models through the platform's ecosystem.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and upcoming developments in the field, offering insights into what lies ahead for artificial intelligence innovation and advancement.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels are leveraging Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing operations.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft open sources its 'farm of the future' toolkit, enabling developers to build AI-powered agricultural solutions for sustainable farming practices.

articles.title: Celebrating 20 years of Google Translate: Fun facts, tips and new features to try
articles.summary: Google Translate celebrates 20 years, evolving from a 2006 AI experiment to supporting nearly 250 languages. The company shares fun facts highlighting the service's growth and new features.

articles.title: Join the new AI Agents Vibe Coding Course from Google and Kaggle
articles.summary: Google and Kaggle are offering a 5-Day AI Agents Intensive Course with registration now open for interested participants.

articles.title: 8 Gemini tips for organizing your space (and life)
articles.summary: Gemini offers AI-powered tips for organizing both physical and digital spaces, including cleaning schedules, inbox decluttering, and seasonal chore management.

articles.title: Enabling a new model for healthcare with AI co-clinician
articles.summary: Researchers are developing an AI co-clinician to augment healthcare delivery, exploring how AI can partner with medical professionals to improve patient care outcomes.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with South Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using advanced AI models. The collaboration aims to leverage frontier artificial intelligence technology for research advancement.

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
articles.summary: Decoupled DiLoCo enables resilient distributed AI training by allowing independent local training on separate compute clusters that periodically synchronize. This approach improves fault tolerance and efficiency in large-scale model training.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #242 - ChatGPT Images 2.0, Qwen 3.6 Max, Kimi-K2.6
articles.summary: OpenAI released ChatGPT Images 2.0 with improved text and screenshot generation. The episode discusses this along with updates on Qwen 3.6 Max and Kimi-K2.6.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. The episode discusses these releases alongside other major AI news including Mistral's Small 4 model family and developments in attention mechanisms.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launches DLSS 5, a generative AI technology that enhances video game graphics in real-time by boosting photorealism. The technology has potential applications beyond gaming.

articles.title: What LG and NVIDIA’s talks reveal about the future of physical AI
articles.summary: LG and NVIDIA are exploring partnerships in physical AI, data centers, and mobility, revealing the significant infrastructure and capital requirements needed to deploy autonomous systems beyond simulation environments.

articles.title: A guide to APIs, MCPs, and MCP Gateways
articles.summary: APIs enable communication between software applications, while MCPs allow large language models to access data and tools in structured ways. Both facilitate information exchange but serve different purposes in their respective systems.

articles.title: AI agent governance takes focus as regulators flag control gaps
articles.summary: Australia's financial regulator warns that AI agent governance in banks and financial firms lacks maturity, with boards insufficiently scrutinizing risks like unpredictable AI behavior and operational failures.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, advancing debugging of collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduces Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses key challenges in complex indoor environments by improving target localization, self-localization, and path planning.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study reveals AI coding assistants like Claude Code are publishing sensitive API keys to public code repositories, creating security risks.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that exposes millions of AI applications to remote takeovers, treating a dangerous vulnerability as expected behavior.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distillation optimizes LLMs for speed but sacrifices generalization ability and problem-solving flexibility on complex tasks.

articles.title: Anthropic's new Claude Security tool scans your codebase for flaws - and helps you decide what to fix first
articles.summary: Anthropic released Claude Security, a tool using Opus 4.7 to scan codebases for vulnerabilities, validate findings, and generate patches. It prioritizes dangerous flaws to prevent exploitation.

articles.title: Sony vs. Bose: My buying advice after listening to flagship headphones from both brands
articles.summary: Sony and Bose flagship headphones are compared, revealing their respective strengths and weaknesses to help consumers make informed purchasing decisions.

articles.title: T-Mobile will give you the latest Apple Watch SE for free - how to qualify for the deal
articles.summary: T-Mobile offers the new Apple Watch SE 3 for free when customers add a new line to their account, providing a cost-saving opportunity for those looking to upgrade their wearables.

articles.title: Effective KV Compression with TurboQuant
articles.summary: Google launched TurboQuant, an algorithmic suite for quantizing and compressing large language models and vector search engines, essential for RAG systems.

articles.title: Building AI Agents in Python with Pydantic AI
articles.summary: Pydantic AI enables developers to build intelligent agents in Python using structured data validation. The framework simplifies agent creation with type safety and validation features.

articles.title: Effective Context Engineering for AI Agents: A Developer’s Guide
articles.summary: Guide for developers on optimizing context engineering in AI agents to improve performance and reliability. Focuses on practical techniques for structuring and managing information passed to AI systems.

articles.title: A Gentle Introduction to Stochastic Programming
articles.summary: Stochastic programming helps make optimal decisions when future data is uncertain. It addresses the limitation of relying on inaccurate spreadsheet predictions for planning.

articles.title: Proxy-Pointer RAG: Multimodal Answers Without Multimodal Embeddings
articles.summary: Proxy-Pointer RAG enables multimodal answers without requiring multimodal embeddings, using structure as a key approach for efficient processing.

articles.title: How to Study the Monotonicity and Stability of Variables in a Scoring Model using Python
articles.summary: Learn how to validate variable consistency in scoring models using Python by studying monotonicity and stability to ensure reliable risk assessment.

articles.title: It’s Gonna Be May: 16 Games Hit the Cloud This Month, With More NVIDIA GeForce RTX 5080 Power
articles.summary: 16 games are launching on NVIDIA's GeForce NOW in May, including AAA titles like Forza Horizon 6 and 007 First Light on day one. The Ultimate membership offers expanded RTX 5080 cloud gaming power for streaming PC games across devices.

articles.title: NVIDIA Launches Nemotron 3 Nano Omni Model, Unifying Vision, Audio and Language for up to 9x More Efficient AI Agents
articles.summary: NVIDIA launched Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, a unified multimodal model combining vision, audio, and language capabilities into one system, enabling 9x more efficient AI agents with faster responses and advanced reasoning.

articles.title: Into the Omniverse: Manufacturing’s Simulation-First Era Has Arrived
articles.summary: Manufacturing is entering a simulation-first era where virtual testing replaces traditional real-world testing cycles. NVIDIA Omniverse and OpenUSD enable developers to transform workflows through advanced digital simulation.

articles.title: Sun Finance automates ID extraction and fraud detection with generative AI on AWS
articles.summary: Sun Finance uses generative AI on AWS to automate ID extraction and fraud detection, reducing manual review from 60% to minimal levels. The solution processes millions of loan applications monthly with improved accuracy and efficiency.

articles.title: Unleashing Agentic AI Analytics on Amazon SageMaker with Amazon Athena and Amazon Quick
articles.summary: Amazon Quick's agentic AI transforms data analytics into self-service capability, enabling business users to query complex datasets through natural language interfaces without requiring specialized technical expertise.

articles.title: Configuring Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Gateway for secure access to private resources
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore now supports VPC connectivity for secure access to private resources, enabling AI agents to reach internal APIs and databases without exposing traffic to the public internet.

articles.title: Build AI-Powered Games with NVIDIA DLSS 4.5, RTX, and Unreal Engine 5
articles.summary: NVIDIA releases DLSS 4.5 with Dynamic Multi Frame Generation and enhanced Super Resolution for game developers using Unreal Engine 5, enabling AI-powered graphics improvements.

articles.title: How to Build, Run, and Scale High-Quality Creator Workflows in ComfyUI
articles.summary: ComfyUI is an open-source, node-based tool that helps creative teams automate asset production using generative AI, compressing manual tasks into efficient, repeatable workflows.

articles.title: Automating GPU Kernel Translation with AI Agents: cuTile Python to cuTile.jl
articles.summary: NVIDIA's cuTile is a tile-based GPU programming model that AI agents can automatically translate from Python to Julia, simplifying kernel development by abstracting low-level operations.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: Study examines generational differences in consumer attitudes toward social media advertising across various platforms, conducted in collaboration with CrowdDNA.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint and sharing it openly with others.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with unconventional background innovations cutting-edge extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences drive breakthrough developments in XR field.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for world models enabling long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, state-space stochasticity for exploration, and improved gradient shaping for vision-based control.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers are developing methods to identify feature interactions in large language models at scale, advancing interpretability research to make AI decision-making more transparent and trustworthy.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: This research presents an information-driven approach to designing imaging systems that optimizes how well measurements distinguish objects using only noisy data and noise models.
